title: Knocking Out a Creature
excerpt: Knocking out a creature is a nonlethal combat resolution that leaves the target unconscious instead of dead when an appropriate attack or effect reduces it to the required threshold. It matters when a conflict is being resolved by capture, restraint, or removal without a lethal finish.
markdown:
# Knocking Out a Creature

Knocking out a creature is a nonlethal outcome that replaces a lethal defeat when a qualifying attack or effect reduces the target to the required point in combat resolution. It is used when the goal is to disable a creature without killing it.

## Rules

- Knocking a creature out is a nonlethal resolution rather than a separate damage type
- It becomes relevant when an appropriate attack or effect would reduce the target to the defeat threshold that would otherwise end the fight lethally
- The target is left alive but disabled, typically by entering the [[unconscious_condition|Unconscious]] state until another rule changes that outcome
- Whether a creature can be knocked out depends on the kind of attack, effect, and governing combat rule involved

## Combat Resolution

### Nonlethal Finish
When a qualifying attack reduces a creature to the relevant threshold, the resolving side can produce an unconscious outcome instead of a lethal one.

### Damage Still Resolves First
The attack or effect still resolves through ordinary [[damage|Damage]] and [[hit_points|Hit Points]] handling. The knockout choice changes the final outcome of the defeat, not the fact that the attack connected.

### Appropriate Source Matters
Knocking out a creature depends on the kind of attack or effect being used. Close-quarters physical attacks are the most common source, while other effects may not support a nonlethal finish unless a rule says they do.

## Common Uses

- Ending a fight by capture instead of killing
- Dropping a guard, witness, or rival alive for later interaction
- Resolving a melee victory as incapacitation rather than death
